2603.23844.title: Language Model Planners do not Scale, but do Formalizers?
2603.23844.paper_summary: Recent work shows overwhelming evidence that LLMs, even those trained to scale their reasoning trace, perform unsatisfactorily when solving planning problems too complex. Whether the same conclusion holds for LLM formalizers that generate solver-oriented programs remains unknown. We systematically show that LLM formalizers greatly out-scale LLM planners, some retaining perfect accuracy in the classic BlocksWorld domain with a huge state space of size up to $10^{165}$. While performance of smaller LLM formalizers degrades with problem complexity, we show that a divide-and-conquer formalizing technique can greatly improve its robustness. Finally, we introduce unraveling problems where one line of problem description realistically corresponds to exponentially many lines of formal language such as the Planning Domain Definition Language (PDDL), greatly challenging LLM formalizers. We tackle this challenge by introducing a new paradigm, namely LLM-as-higher-order-formalizer, where an LLM generates a program generator. This decouples token output from the combinatorial explosion of the underlying formalization and search space.
2603.23844.paper_summary_zh: 最近的研究顯示，LLMs，即使是那些訓練以擴展其推理痕跡的模型，在解決過於複雜的規劃問題時表現不佳。對於生成解算器導向程序的LLM形式化工具是否也會得出相同結論，目前仍然未知。我們系統地顯示，LLM形式化工具在規模上遠超LLM規劃工具，其中一些在經典的BlocksWorld領域中保持完美的準確性，該領域的狀態空間大小可達$10^{165}$。雖然較小的LLM形式化工具在問題複雜性上表現下降，但我們顯示一種分而治之的形式化技術可以大大提高其穩健性。最後，我們引入了展開問題，其中一行問題描述實際上對應於指數多行形式語言，如規劃領域定義語言（PDDL），這對LLM形式化工具提出了巨大挑戰。我們通過引入一種新範式來應對這一挑戰，即LLM作為高階形式化工具，其中LLM生成一個程序生成器。這將標記輸出與基礎形式化和搜索空間的組合爆炸解耦。
2603.23844.author: Owen Jiang et.al.
2603.23844.authors: Owen Jiang, Cassie Huang, Ashish Sabharwal, Li Zhang
